text:

Europe’s software market in search of scale and niches. [Analysis](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/analyses/index.html). [Technology, Media & Telecom](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/technologie/index.html). 25/09/2025 • 8 minutes read. [Julia Krauwer](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/auteurs/julia-krauwer/index.html). Europe’s technology sector struggles to scale potential winners due to a lack of growth capital.

metadata.tags: Analyse, Technologie, Media & Telecom, AI, Digitalisering
metadata.author: Julia Krauwer
metadata.description: Europe’s SaaS market brims with innovation but struggles to scale. Discover the opportunities in AI SaaS and other niches.
metadata.parent_text:
![](https://assets.abnamro.com/api/public/content/shutterstock-1675867219-landscape-4x1.jpg?t=h440)
Europe’s software market in search of scale and niches. [Analysis](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/analyses/index.html). [Technology, Media & Telecom](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/technologie/index.html). 25/09/2025 • 8 minutes read. [Julia Krauwer](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/auteurs/julia-krauwer/index.html). Europe’s technology sector struggles to scale potential winners due to a lack of growth capital. Yet the continent has a broad range of innovative players. In the Netherlands, almost two-thirds of investment in young software companies goes to parties that apply artificial intelligence. In 2024, young European technology companies collectively raised about 54 billion dollars in venture capital. Almost half of that, over 23 billion, flowed to Software-as-a-Service (SaaS). This makes SaaS the largest category, bigger than digital marketplaces or companies developing physical technology. For investors, the combination of stable subscriptions and scalability is particularly attractive. During the coronavirus pandemic, abundant capital flowed into technology, helped by low interest rates and investors’ search for scalable business models. SaaS benefited above average.

metadata.tags: Analyse, Technologie, Media & Telecom, AI, Digitalisering
metadata.author: Julia Krauwer
metadata.description: Europe’s SaaS market brims with innovation but struggles to scale. Discover the opportunities in AI SaaS and other niches.
metadata.parent_text: Within Europe, the United Kingdom attracts the most SaaS investment with over 77 billion dollars in ten years — more than one-third of the European total. France and Germany follow with 35 billion dollars (16 per cent) and 33 billion (15 per cent) respectively. The Netherlands ranks a steady fourth with 9.9 billion, accounting for 4.5 per cent of all European SaaS investment between 2015 and 2025. Unfortunately, Europe faces a persistent problem: the lack of capital for tech companies to grow into internationally competitive businesses. US start-ups are twice as likely to raise a round of more than 15 million dollars as their European counterparts, according to the 2024 [State of European Tech report](https://www.stateofeuropeantech.com/chapters/investment-levels). As a result, the share of young companies that manage to break through to the scale-up phase is much smaller in Europe. According to the researchers, the cumulative shortfall now amounts to 375 billion dollars — the total amount that young tech companies would have raised over the past ten years if access to larger investments had been as easy as in the United States (US). In the US, a large share of the money even goes to rounds above 100 million dollars, with a dominant role for megadeals above 250 million. Although Europe does not even close that many fewer SaaS deals — last year there were 4,616, versus 5,040 in the US — the centre of gravity remains with smaller rounds. Broadly applicable SaaS niches. Venture capital in Europe mainly flows to several recognisable SaaS niches.

text: The largest category is enterprise software — solutions that make organisations’ internal processes more efficient, from HR and planning systems to workflow and ERP tools. For example, German unicorn Personio further strengthened its position in HR software, and compatriot N8N specialises in process automation.

id: Europa’s softwaremarkt op zoek naar schaal en niches__a9-hybrid-small-to-big__0044
text: The development is even faster in the US; there, AI SaaS absorbs almost 80 per cent of all SaaS funding. Even so, Europe produces many innovative, AI-first SaaS companies that specialise in all sorts of niches.

metadata.chunk_id: Europa’s softwaremarkt op zoek naar schaal en niches__a9-hybrid-small-to-big__0044
metadata.source_slug: Europa’s softwaremarkt op zoek naar schaal en niches
metadata.source_title: Europe’s software market in search of scale and niches
metadata.tags: Analyse, Technologie, Media & Telecom, AI, Digitalisering
metadata.author: Julia Krauwer
metadata.description: Europe’s SaaS market brims with innovation but struggles to scale. Discover the opportunities in AI SaaS and other niches.
metadata.parent_text: The development is even faster in the US; there, AI SaaS absorbs almost 80 per cent of all SaaS funding. Even so, Europe produces many innovative, AI-first SaaS companies that specialise in all sorts of niches. The Netherlands even has an above-average number of AI SaaS deals and funding. Cradle, for example, developed an AI model that can design new proteins and sells it as a SaaS service to laboratories. Fellow Dutch company Fourthline uses AI for identity fraud and compliance in the financial sector, and FRISS does the same for insurance risks. TestGorilla makes selection processes smarter and less sensitive to bias, and Sympower uses AI to keep electricity grids in balance. Germany’s Accure offers a platform for AI analysis of battery data in sectors including mobility and industry, to improve battery and accumulator safety and longevity. UK-based Synthesia uses generative AI to automate the production of corporate films and training videos and raised 180 million dollars in early 2025 at a valuation of over 2 billion. And from Sweden, Lovable is experiencing enormous growth with a platform that uses AI to accelerate and democratise software development. The company was founded in 2023 and reached a valuation of almost 2 billion dollars in a few years.

The company was founded in 2023 and reached a valuation of almost 2 billion dollars in a few years.

text: Major AI developers set the tone. A large share of investment is concentrated in a few major AI developers that set the tone in the market with megadeals.

metadata.source_slug: Europa’s softwaremarkt op zoek naar schaal en niches

metadata.tags: Analyse, Technologie, Media & Telecom, AI, Digitalisering
metadata.author: Julia Krauwer
metadata.description: Europe’s SaaS market brims with innovation but struggles to scale. Discover the opportunities in AI SaaS and other niches.
metadata.parent_text: Major AI developers set the tone. A large share of investment is concentrated in a few major AI developers that set the tone in the market with megadeals. Their AI systems are offered in two ways: as a building block for software developers to embed AI in their own product, and in the form of chat apps such as ChatGPT (OpenAI) and Claude (Anthropic) for end users. Those who want access to the most powerful features pay a monthly subscription. This makes the revenue model essentially the same as SaaS: software offered via the cloud for a periodic fee.

metadata.parent_text: Success factors. Amid the growth power of these major players, SaaS companies need to position themselves sharply. A key trait of promising providers is that they focus on critical business processes — parts of an organisation that are simply crucial. Fourthline, for example, automates the mandatory identification of customers in the financial sector. Banks must perform this process to comply with the law, and switching to another provider carries risks. FRISS does the same for insurers, who have a strong interest in recognising fraudulent claims.

text: Sympower has developed an AI solution that balances electricity grids, and connects both major consumers and energy producers. Collaboration with parties in energy-intensive industries such as agriculture, cement production and data centres requires strong relationships and a great deal of domain knowledge that is not easy to copy.

This allows users to automate complex work processes across different applications.

metadata.parent_text: The road ahead. Europe’s SaaS market brims with innovation, but the road to global dominance is anything but straightforward. The continent suffers from a structural scalability gap and a more cautious investment culture. However, encouraged by a geopolitical need to become more self-sufficient technologically as well, it is taking several steps in the right direction. On 23 September 2025, Minister Karremans presented a list of [measures](https://www.rijksoverheid.nl/actueel/nieuws/2025/09/23/meer-groei-nederlandse-startups-en-scale-ups-door-meer-kapitaal-talent-en-verlenging-techleap) to support “the tech champions of tomorrow”, with an emphasis on research-intensive key technologies such as cybersecurity, quantum computing and artificial intelligence.
